title: Kheer (Rice Pudding)

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Full-fat milk – 2 liters (preferably cow’s milk for richer flavor)
Basmati rice – 80 g (about ½ cup; aged basmati works best)
Sugar – 150–180 g (¾–1 cup, adjust to taste)
Saffron strands – a pinch (soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk)

instruction.title: Step 1: Prep the Rice
steps: Rinse the basmati rice gently until water runs clear to remove excess starch.
Soak for 20–30 minutes, then drain completely.

instruction.title: Step 2: Infuse the Milk
steps: In a heavy-bottomed pan, bring milk to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce to low simmer. Scrape down cream from sides and stir back into milk occasionally — this develops richness.

instruction.title: Step 3: Cook the Rice
steps: Heat ghee in a small pan, lightly sauté drained rice for 2–3 minutes until aromatic.
Add rice to simmering milk. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. Cook until grains are soft and begin to release starch (about 20–25 min).

instruction.title: Step 4: Sweeten & Flavor
steps: Add sugar and crushed cardamom. Continue simmering until kheer thickens to a creamy consistency (another 15–20 min).

instruction.title: Step 5: Finish with Garnish
steps: In ghee, lightly fry cashews until golden, then add almonds, pistachios, and raisins briefly.
Stir fried nuts & raisins into the kheer.
Turn off heat and add rose water if using.

instruction.title: Step 6: Serving & Storage
steps: Serve warm for comfort or chilled for a refreshing dessert.
Store in refrigerator up to 3 days. The kheer will thicken further upon cooling — loosen with warm milk before serving if needed.

chefTips: Slow simmer only — boiling aggressively can split the milk and dull flavor.
Don’t skip side-scraping — the malai (milk cream) scraped from the sides enriches texture.
Balance saffron — too much can overpower the delicate sweetness.
Age improves flavor — kheer tastes even better the next day as the spices infuse.
